TimeEntry.Notes: got a call from Melissa regarding one drive issue her system. So connected to her system and perform the trouble shoot step but did not work during the trouble shooting she ask she is leave at 2am  please work on after hours so I leave the computer and so ticket assign to after hour shift

TimeEntry.Title: Melissa OneDrive PDF Print Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Melissa was unable to print a PDF file stored on OneDrive. During troubleshooting, it was found that the issue was caused by a problem with her OneDrive connection. The OneDrive app was reset, and it was noticed that multiple accounts were logged in under OneDrive access and school settings. The unnecessary accounts were removed, and Melissa tried to sign in again. The issue remained because she was logged in with a different account that did not have access to the required PDF file. After correcting the account login, OneDrive started syncing properly, and the PDF could be accessed.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Sync Issue – DESKTOP-I7G1MM7 & DESKTOP-N6E84SS
TimeEntry.Notes:
Identified a syncing issue in ManageEngine for the devices DESKTOP-I7G1MM7 and DESKTOP-N6E84SS. The systems were not properly synchronizing with the ManageEngine server, causing delays in reporting and updates.

Actions Taken:

  • Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified the sync status for both affected machines.

  • Checked communication between the endpoint agent and the ManageEngine server.

  • Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish connectivity and resolve synchronization errors.

  • Re-initiated sync and confirmed successful communication.

  • Verified in ManageEngine that both devices are now syncing properly and reporting as expected.

Current Status:
The sync issue has been successfully resolved. Both devices (DESKTOP-I7G1MM7 and DESKTOP-N6E84SS) are now syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: AAVA- Printer error
TimeEntry.Notes:
AAVA – Printer Error (A9 Code)

Ticket Summary:

Received a call from the user regarding a printer issue. Connected to the user’s system remotely and reinstalled the printer driver — printing functionality was restored initially.

After some time, the scanner displayed error A9. Attempted multiple troubleshooting steps, including:

  • Resetting the printer

  • Retesting scan functionality

  • Reviewing documentation and manufacturer resources

As per the manufacturer’s documentation, error A9 indicates a hardware malfunction that requires vendor support.

Action Taken:

  • Informed the user about the hardware issue.

  • Advised the user to contact the printer manufacturer for further assistance.

  • User confirmed they will contact the manufacturer.

Status: Closed – Hardware issue escalated to manufacturer.

TimeEntry.Title: Got a call from Melisa regarding the printer issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a call from Melisa regarding an issue with her printer. I connected to her computer remotely to investigate the problem. I first checked the printer's IP settings, confirmed the printer was powered on, and ran the print spooler service. I attempted to ping the printer's IP, but the issue persisted.

To further troubleshoot, I ran an IP scanner to detect the printer's IP address but could not find it. I then pinged all the parameters, but the host was unreachable. Despite these efforts, the printer still could not connect, and the actual IP of the printer could not be confirmed.

I advised the user to turn the printer off and back on, but the issue remained unresolved. For further troubleshooting, I handed the case over to another technician to continue the resolution process.

TimeEntry.Title: Got a call from Melisa regarding the printer issue
TimeEntry.Notes: User had issue connecting to the printer, Assisted from a video call. User had an issue with the port, Cox assisted them few days ago, since then the printer is down. Issue needs a visit. User will schedule the visit after checking with Cox.

so went to the chrome settings remove the unwanted search engines and make google as the default on on both the browsers...

verify the same settings on other device as well......hence closing the ticket....
